title: The Army Is Burning Through Its AI Tokens - Slashdot
excerpt: An anonymous reader quotes a report from Wired: A little over a month after the Department of Defense (DOD) bragged that nearly half of its 3.5 million employees were using AI at work, members of the Army's Combat Capabilities Development Command (DEVCOM) received an email informing them that they w...
content_html:
An anonymous reader quotes a report from Wired: A little over a month after the Department of Defense (DOD) bragged that nearly half of its 3.5 million employees were using AI at work, members of the Army's Combat Capabilities Development Command (DEVCOM) received an email informing them that they were burning through tokens, and needed to limit use. "Although the Army CIO announced in May 2026 that they were offering unlimited tokens, by mid-June the Army CIO pool was exhausted of tokens and had to re-establish limits," the email reads. The email goes on to say that although the Army has chosen to renew token usage at "its current levels," it's unclear "if the Army CIO pool will be renewed after 1 Oct."

The Army uses Ask Sage, a multimodal generative AI platform where users can run different large language models (LLMs), including Alphabet's Gemini, Meta's Llama, and OpenAI's ChatGPT. "Apparently the whole Army burned through the whole year of tokens for just one service," says an Army employee who spoke to WIRED [...]. The Army employee says that the Army has been pushing its workers to lean into using generative AI. Employees were given an allotment of at least 200,000 tokens per month, according to emails viewed by WIRED, and were automatically allocated more if they burned through their initial allotment. Employees who had signed up for Ask Sage but were not regularly using it would receive emails encouraging them to use more of their allocated tokens.

In order to use Ask Sage, the Army had access to 100,000,000 tokens as part of an annual subscription to an "enterprise pack." Tokens represent a unit of output, either in text or image, from an LLM. For the Ask Sage tool, a single token equates to about 3.7 characters, according to documents viewed by WIRED. The Defense Department burned through some 20 billion tokens per day during the 38-day Operation Epic Fury in Iran, according to Breaking Defense. It's unclear if the tokens used by regular DOD employees are drawn from the same pool as those who might be using AI tools on classified or secret information.

"Although the Army CIO announced in May 2026 that they were offering unlimited tokens, by mid-June the Army CIO pool was exhausted of tokens and had to re-establish limits," the email reads. The email goes on to say that although the Army has chosen to renew token usage at "its current levels," it's unclear "if the Army CIO pool will be renewed after 1 Oct."

The Army uses Ask Sage, a multimodal generative AI platform where users can run different large language models (LLMs), including Alphabet's Gemini, Meta's Llama, and OpenAI's ChatGPT. "Apparently the whole Army burned through the whole year of tokens for just one service," says an Army employee who spoke to WIRED [...]. The Army employee says that the Army has been pushing its workers to lean into using generative AI. Employees were given an allotment of at least 200,000 tokens per month, according to emails viewed by WIRED, and were automatically allocated more if they burned through their initial allotment. In order to use Ask Sage, the Army had access to 100,000,000 tokens as part of an annual subscription to an "enterprise pack." Tokens represent a unit of output, either in text or image, from an LLM. For the Ask Sage tool, a single token equates to about 3.7 characters, according to documents viewed by WIRED. The Defense Department burned through some 20 billion tokens per day during the 38-day Operation Epic Fury in Iran, according to Breaking Defense. It's unclear if the tokens used by regular DOD employees are drawn from the same pool as those who might be using AI tools on classified or secret information.

sentences.en: An anonymous reader quotes a report from Wired: A little over a month after the Department of Defense (DOD) bragged that nearly half of its 3.5 million employees were using AI at work, members of the Army's Combat Capabilities Development Command (DEVCOM) received an email informing them that they were burning through tokens, and needed to limit use.
sentences.zh: 一位匿名读者引用了《连线》杂志的一篇报道：在国防部（DOD）吹嘘其 350 万名员工中近一半在工作中使用人工智能后一个多月，陆军作战能力发展司令部（DEVCOM）的成员收到一封电子邮件，通知他们正在销毁代币，需要限制使用。

sentences.en: "Although the Army CIO announced in May 2026 that they were offering unlimited tokens, by mid-June the Army CIO pool was exhausted of tokens and had to re-establish limits," the email reads.
sentences.zh: 电子邮件中写道：“尽管陆军 CIO 在 2026 年 5 月宣布他们将提供无限的代币，但到 6 月中旬，陆军 CIO 池的代币已经耗尽，不得不重新设定限制。”

sentences.en: The email goes on to say that although the Army has chosen to renew token usage at "its current levels," it's unclear "if the Army CIO pool will be renewed after 1 Oct." The Army uses Ask Sage, a multimodal generative AI platform where users can run different large language models (LLMs), including Alphabet's Gemini, Meta's Llama, and OpenAI's ChatGPT.
sentences.zh: 该电子邮件接着说，尽管陆军选择以“当前水平”更新代币使用，但尚不清楚“陆军 CIO 池是否会在 10 月 1 日后更新”。陆军使用 Ask Sage，这是一个多模式生成人工智能平台，用户可以在其中运行不同的大型语言模型 (LLM)，包括 Alphabet 的 Gemini、Meta 的 Llama 和 OpenAI 的 ChatGPT。

sentences.en: "Apparently the whole Army burned through the whole year of tokens for just one service," says an Army employee who spoke to WIRED [...].
sentences.zh: 一位接受《连线》杂志采访的陆军员工表示：“显然，整个陆军为了一项服务就烧毁了一整年的代币。”

sentences.en: The Army employee says that the Army has been pushing its workers to lean into using generative AI.
sentences.zh: 这名陆军员工表示，陆军一直在推动其工作人员倾向于使用生成式人工智能。

sentences.en: Employees were given an allotment of at least 200,000 tokens per month, according to emails viewed by WIRED, and were automatically allocated more if they burned through their initial allotment.
sentences.zh: 根据《连线》杂志查看的电子邮件，员工每月获得至少 200,000 个代币的分配，如果他们用完最初的分配，则会自动分配更多代币。

sentences.en: Employees who had signed up for Ask Sage but were not regularly using it would receive emails encouraging them to use more of their allocated tokens.
sentences.zh: 已注册 Ask Sage 但不经常使用的员工会收到电子邮件，鼓励他们使用更多分配的代币。

sentences.en: In order to use Ask Sage, the Army had access to 100,000,000 tokens as part of an annual subscription to an "enterprise pack."
sentences.zh: 为了使用 Ask Sage，陆军可以获得 100,000,000 个代币，作为“企业包”年度订阅的一部分。

sentences.en: Tokens represent a unit of output, either in text or image, from an LLM.
sentences.zh: 令牌代表法学硕士的文本或图像输出单位。

sentences.en: For the Ask Sage tool, a single token equates to about 3.7 characters, according to documents viewed by WIRED.
sentences.zh: 根据 WIRED 查看的文档，对于 Ask Sage 工具，单个标记相当于大约 3.7 个字符。

sentences.en: The Defense Department burned through some 20 billion tokens per day during the 38-day Operation Epic Fury in Iran, according to Breaking Defense.
sentences.zh: 据 Breaking Defense 报道，在伊朗为期 38 天的史诗愤怒行动中，国防部每天烧毁约 200 亿枚代币。

sentences.en: It's unclear if the tokens used by regular DOD employees are drawn from the same pool as those who might be using AI tools on classified or secret information.
sentences.zh: 目前尚不清楚国防部普通员工使用的代币是否与那些可能在机密或秘密信息上使用人工智能工具的代币来自同一个池。

sentences.zh: 目前尚不清楚国防部普通员工使用的代币是否与那些可能在机密或秘密信息上使用人工智能工具的代币来自同一个池。

content_html_zh: 一位匿名读者引用了《连线》杂志的一篇报道：在国防部（DOD）吹嘘其 350 万名员工中近一半在工作中使用人工智能后一个多月，陆军作战能力发展司令部（DEVCOM）的成员收到一封电子邮件，通知他们正在销毁代币，需要限制使用。 电子邮件中写道：“尽管陆军 CIO 在 2026 年 5 月宣布他们将提供无限的代币，但到 6 月中旬，陆军 CIO 池的代币已经耗尽，不得不重新设定限制。” 该电子邮件接着说，尽管陆军选择以“当前水平”更新代币使用，但尚不清楚“陆军 CIO 池是否会在 10 月 1 日后更新”。陆军使用 Ask Sage，这是一个多模式生成人工智能平台，用户可以在其中运行不同的大型语言模型 (LLM)，包括 Alphabet 的 Gemini、Meta 的 Llama 和 OpenAI 的 ChatGPT。 一位接受《连线》杂志采访的陆军员工表示：“显然，整个陆军为了一项服务就烧毁了一整年的代币。” 这名陆军员工表示，陆军一直在推动其工作人员倾向于使用生成式人工智能。 根据《连线》杂志查看的电子邮件，员工每月获得至少 200,000 个代币的分配，如果他们用完最初的分配，则会自动分配更多代币。 已注册 Ask Sage 但不经常使用的员工会收到电子邮件，鼓励他们使用更多分配的代币。 为了使用 Ask Sage，陆军可以获得 100,000,000 个代币，作为“企业包”年度订阅的一部分。 令牌代表法学硕士的文本或图像输出单位。 根据 WIRED 查看的文档，对于 Ask Sage 工具，单个标记相当于大约 3.7 个字符。 据 Breaking Defense 报道，在伊朗为期 38 天的史诗愤怒行动中，国防部每天烧毁约 200 亿枚代币。 目前尚不清楚国防部普通员工使用的代币是否与那些可能在机密或秘密信息上使用人工智能工具的代币来自同一个池。
